svg.title: Financial Soundness Map
svg.desc: Four dimensions of financial health for an early venture: economic viability, free cash flow, profit-centric view, and new venture economics.
svg.text: Financial Soundness
Cash is the lifeblood of the venture. Four dimensions to understand it before you need it.
Economic Viability
Can the model create profit at all?
Gross margin · payback period · LTV · CAC
Is there a unit economics story that works?
Revenue model: subscription / usage / setup / success / hybrid
Free Cash Flow
Collect early, pay later?
Cash conversion cycle — how many days between paying suppliers and receiving from customers?
Working capital requirements
Where does cash get stuck?
Profit-Centric View
Gross margin per transaction or cohort
Churn rate and its cost impact
Customer acquisition cost vs lifetime value
Is growth funded by cash or by margin?
New Venture Economics
Runway — months of cash left
Burn rate and how it changes with growth
Funding need for next milestone
Default Alive or Default Dead by when?
Founder question: Do we know where the cash actually goes? Can we trace every dollar?
